role: Second Deputy Speaker of Parliament, 10th Majlis (2016–2019); MP for Tehran (2008–2020)
bio: Ali Motahari is the son of the martyred Islamic scholar [Morteza Motahhari](/people/morteza-motahhari) and the brother-in-law of former [Majlis](/orgs/islamic-consultative-assembly) Speaker [Ali Larijani](/people/ali-larijani). He represented Tehran for three consecutive terms (8th–10th Majlis, 2008–2020), serving as Second Deputy Speaker in the 10th Majlis (2016–2019). A principlist by formal affiliation, he was widely regarded as a maverick who publicly challenged [Ahmadinejad](/people/mahmoud-ahmadinejad), criticised [IRGC](/orgs/islamic-revolutionary-guard-corps) political interference, and called for the release of [Green Movement](/events/green-movement-protests-2009) leaders. He was disqualified by the [Guardian Council](/orgs/guardian-council) from the 2020 elections. He holds a PhD in philosophy from the University of Tehran.
editorial_notes: Born 26 January 1958. Disqualified from 2020 election. Son of Morteza Motahhari. Sister Farideh married Ali Larijani, making him Larijani's brother-in-law.
